posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked: Have had a few bottles of 2012 Francois Carillon Bourgogne Blanc of late. It is in a terrific place. The fruit is ripe with a gentle tropical edge but it is not over-blown. It is flecked with mineral and has tangy grapefruit acidity on the finish.

posts.name: Jerry Hey
posts.cooked: I had the 2012 Roulot Bourgogne Blanc a couple days ago. Those two wines can compete with many in a couple tiers higher.

posts.name: Ch@rlie F|_|
posts.cooked: i’m really enjoying the francois stuff which seems a little more available than jacques.

posts.name: Kent Comley
posts.cooked: I have a good mate who bought 3 or 4 doz F Carillon Puligny Village as he reckons it is the best QPR white burg on the planet. He has a palate I trust and yet I am yet to buy any…this will be rectified.

posts.name: Craig Gleason
posts.cooked: We had a 2007 Louis Carillon Puligny Perrieres at Ma Cuisine two weeks ago. Probably the best white of our trip, though Pierre Boisson’s 2013 Auxey-Dresses En Reugne and 1998 Leflaive Pucelles were pretty stiff competition.

posts.name: Max Kelly
posts.cooked: Can anyone recommend a reasonably available (in the US market) Carillon bottling that would exemplify his style? I’m I bought a single bottle of his '14 Folatieres, but don’t want to commit infanticide to see if I should be on the hunt for a few more.

posts.name: Ryan Caughey
posts.cooked:
Max K:

Can anyone recommend a reasonably available (in the US market) Carillon bottling that would exemplify his style? I’m I bought a single bottle of his '14 Folatieres, but don’t want to commit infanticide to see if I should be on the hunt for a few more.

The basic Puligny. Has long been one of the best (or the best) Puligny AOC

posts.name: Max Kelly
posts.cooked: ^ Awesome - thanks! Any idea if the '14 is showing well, or should I hunt down a '12 or '10 (assuming '13 may be closed, and '11 a weaker vintage).

posts.name: Howard Cooper
posts.cooked:
Max K:

Can anyone recommend a reasonably available (in the US market) Carillon bottling that would exemplify his style? I’m I bought a single bottle of his '14 Folatieres, but don’t want to commit infanticide to see if I should be on the hunt for a few more.

Just to be clear, the old Carillon estate is now two estates - the one discussed here (Francois Carillon) and Jacques Carillon - I think they are both sons of Louis Carillon. I have enjoyed wines by both Francois Carillon and Jacques Carillon, but be careful to get what you are looking for, and, of course, Carillon my wayword son.

posts.name: Max Kelly
posts.cooked: Roger, and tracking. I was thinking of Francois, but I appreciate the point. I don’t think I’ve come across any Jacques Carillon wines.

posts.name: Clayton Wai-Poi
posts.cooked: I have had there 2013 Chassagne and Folatieres recently. Neither was shit down, in fact they were quite fruit forward

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
[ResizeableImage=][/ResizeableImage]

Max K:

Roger, and tracking. I was thinking of Francois, but I appreciate the point. I don’t think I’ve come across any Jacques Carillon wines.

The Jacques Carillon wines are stylistically much closer to the Louis Carillon wines of old, unsurprisingly since Jacques made the later vintages of those wines. François’ wines are notably riper, more fruit-forward and gourmand. They show well young. He also works with more sites, right up to Chevalier Montrachet.



Hard to see any wine from Puligny winning a white Burgundy QPR contest when there are wines like Vincent Dureuil-Janthial’s 2014 Rullys on the market.

posts.name: Mark Golodetz
posts.cooked: Jeremy, far and away your worst pun.

posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked:
Agree with William style wise. Had a '14 Jacques Carillon Puligny with lunch today. Piercing, direct, chalky and complex. Probably the best village Puligny on the market today for mine.

Thank you very little Mark.

Best Regards
Jeremy

posts.name: Brian Buzzini
posts.cooked: Can’t believe with ALL the Carillon wines that I post on…I didn’t come up with “Keep Calm and Carillon”!!! Good one mate!!

posts.name: Craig Gleason
posts.cooked:
B. Buzzini:

Can’t believe with ALL the Carillon wines that I post on…I didn’t come up with “Keep Calm and Carillon”!!! Good one mate!!

Well, with all those screaming eagles, a man can’t hear himself think.

posts.name: Rick Allen
posts.cooked:
Jeremy Holmes:

Have had a few bottles of 2012 Francois Carillon Bourgogne Blanc of late. It is in a terrific place. The fruit is ripe with a gentle tropical edge but it is not over-blown. It is flecked with mineral and has tangy grapefruit acidity on the finish.

We’ve gone through a couple of cases of this. Fantastic value.
